description: Claim Your Home — Pearl Registry Integration. App-side claim flow with mock registry API, document-based ownership verification, data import, and ownership transfer.

userStories.title: Create RegistryHome data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed data models for registry home data so all registry-related code has a shared type foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/models/RegistryHome.ts with interfaces: RegistryHome (id, address, score, claimStatus), ClaimStatus type ('unclaimed' | 'claimed' | 'claimed_by_other' | 'transfer_pending'), RegistryHomeData (score, pillarScores, verifiedFeatures, knownEquipment, climateRisk, energyProjections, neighborhoodComparisons), VerificationDocument (uri, type: 'utility_bill' | 'closing_docs' | 'tax_record', uploadedAt)
RegistryHomeData.verifiedFeatures is array of { name: string; value: string; verified: boolean }
RegistryHomeData.knownEquipment is array of { type: string; make?: string; model?: string; yearInstalled?: number }
RegistryHomeData.climateRisk is { floodZone: string; fireRisk: string; windRisk: string; overallRisk: 'low' | 'moderate' | 'high' }
RegistryHomeData.energyProjections is { annualCostEstimate: number; savingsOpportunity: number }
RegistryHomeData.neighborhoodComparisons is { averageScore: number; percentile: number; totalHomes: number }
Export all types from src/models/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create RegistryApiService interface and MockRegistryService
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed API contract and mock service so the app can be built against registry endpoints before the real backend exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/services/RegistryApiService.ts with interface RegistryApi defining: lookupHome(address: string): Promise<RegistryHome | null>, claimHome(homeId: string, userId: string, verificationDoc: VerificationDocument): Promise<{ success: boolean; homeData?: RegistryHomeData }>, getClaimStatus(homeId: string): Promise<ClaimStatus>, getHomeData(homeId: string): Promise<RegistryHomeData | null>, transferOwnership(homeId: string, newUserId: string, verificationDoc: VerificationDocument): Promise<{ success: boolean; pendingDuration?: string }>, syncAppData(homeId: string, appData: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>
Create src/services/MockRegistryService.ts implementing RegistryApi with realistic mock data
Mock lookupHome returns a home for any of the 4 existing mock addresses (Austin TX) with score 680, realistic pillar scores, 8+ verified features, 4+ known equipment items
Mock claimHome auto-approves after 500ms delay and returns full RegistryHomeData
Mock transferOwnership returns immediate success for utility_bill, pendingDuration '24 hours' for other doc types
Mock syncAppData logs to console after 300ms delay
All mock calls have randomized 300-800ms latency to simulate real API
Export MockRegistryService from src/services/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create RegistryContext with AsyncStorage persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a context provider for registry state so claim status and imported data persist across app restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/context/RegistryContext.tsx following existing context pattern (createContext, Provider, isLoaded gate, useCallback mutators)
State: claimStatus (ClaimStatus), registryHome (RegistryHome | null), registryData (RegistryHomeData | null), isLoading (boolean)
Provides: claimStatus, registryHome, registryData, isLoading, lookupHome(address), claimHome(verificationDoc), transferOwnership(verificationDoc), syncData(appData)
State persisted in AsyncStorage under @pearl_registry_home key
On mount: hydrate from AsyncStorage, then if a home exists, background-refresh claimStatus via getClaimStatus()
Export useRegistry hook and RegistryProvider from src/context/index.ts
Add RegistryProvider to App.tsx provider chain
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add navigation routes for claim flow screens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all new screens wired into the navigation stack so users can reach them.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to RootStackParamList in RootNavigator.tsx: OwnershipVerification: { homeId: string; isTransfer?: boolean }, ClaimConfirmation: { homeId: string }, RegistryDetail: undefined
Add placeholder screen wrapper functions for OwnershipVerificationScreen, ClaimConfirmationScreen (can render empty View with screen name text for now)
Wire all 3 screens into RootNavigator Stack.Screen entries
Back navigation works from all new screens (hardware back + header back button)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add registry lookup and claim CTA to AddressEntryScreen
userStories.description: As a homeowner, when I select my address, I want to see whether this home exists in the Pearl Registry and its current claim status so I know what to expect.
acceptanceCriteria: After address selection and globe zoom completes, call useRegistry().lookupHome(address)
Show loading skeleton card below selected address while lookup runs
If home found + unclaimed: show registry status card with Pearl SCORE badge and 'Found in Pearl Registry' text, plus 'Claim This Home' teal Button below
If home found + claimed by current user: show 'Claimed on Pearl Registry' card with teal checkmark icon
If home found + claimed by other: show amber-bordered card with 'This home has been claimed' and tappable 'Are you the new owner?' link
If home not found or API error: no registry card shown, app continues normally with existing Continue button
'Claim This Home' button navigates to OwnershipVerification screen with homeId param
'Are you the new owner?' link navigates to OwnershipVerification with homeId and isTransfer: true
Messaging uses 'Claim to unlock verified data about your home' — NOT score-improvement language
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build OwnershipVerificationScreen with document upload
userStories.description: As a homeowner claiming my home, I need to upload a document proving I own it so Pearl can verify my ownership.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/screens/OwnershipVerificationScreen.tsx with props: homeId, isTransfer (optional boolean), onVerified callback, onBack callback
Header: back button (48px), centered title 'Verify Ownership', 48px spacer

Document type section: 3 selectable cards — 'Utility Bill' (receipt-outline icon, pre-highlighted with 'Recommended' teal pill badge), 'Closing Documents' (document-outline), 'Property Tax Record' (business-outline)
Selected card has teal border + light teal background; unselected cards have default border
Guidance text above cards: 'Upload a clear photo showing your name and this address'
After doc type selected: show camera capture area reusing BillCaptureScreen pattern (dashed border target, camera icon, 'Or choose from photo library' link)
Uses expo-image-picker for camera and gallery (same launchCameraAsync/launchImageLibraryAsync pattern as BillCaptureScreen)
Photo preview state: full-width Image at 300px height + 'Retake' outline button + 'Submit for Verification' teal button
Submitting state: ActivityIndicator + 'Verifying ownership...' text, buttons disabled
On submit: call useRegistry().claimHome(verificationDoc) or transferOwnership(verificationDoc) if isTransfer
On success: navigate to ClaimConfirmation screen
On error: show 'We couldn't verify this document' message + 'Try Again' button + 'Try a different document' link that resets to doc type selection
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build ClaimConfirmationScreen with data import summary
userStories.description: As a homeowner who just claimed my home, I want to see what Pearl already knows about it and have that data pre-fill my app profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/screens/ClaimConfirmationScreen.tsx with props: homeId, onContinue callback
NO back button in header — this is a terminal success screen, only forward navigation
Celebration section: large teal checkmark icon (64px) with scale-in animation (Animated.spring), 'Your home is now claimed!' h2 text, 'Welcome home!' body text
Data summary Card showing: Pearl SCORE (large, accent color), pillar breakdown (5 small status indicators), count of verified features, count of known equipment items
Collapsible 'What Pearl already knows' section (reuse PillarAssessmentSection chevron pattern) listing each verified feature with checkmark icon + name + value
Non-collapsible 'What you can add' section with plus-circle icon items: 'Add equipment photos', 'Connect smart devices', 'Complete home assessments'
'Continue to My Home' full-width teal Button at bottom with safe area padding
On continue: registry data is merged into local app state via useRegistry context, then navigate to MyHomeScreen
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add claim status badge to MyHomeScreen
userStories.description: As a homeowner, I want to see my home's claim status on MyHomeScreen so I know whether my home is connected to the Pearl Registry.
acceptanceCriteria: Add claim status section below ScoreDisplay on MyHomeScreen, above overall assessment progress
Import useRegistry hook to get claimStatus
Unclaimed state: teal text link with house-outline icon — 'Claim on Pearl Registry →' — tapping navigates to AddressEntryScreen or OwnershipVerification
Claimed state: subtle inline badge with house+checkmark icon (16px, muted teal) + 'Pearl Registry' text (caption, secondary color) — tapping navigates to RegistryDetail
Transfer pending state: amber inline badge with time-outline icon + 'Transfer pending' text (amber color) — tapping shows transfer status
No registry data state: nothing rendered (graceful absence, user unaware of registry)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wire navigation callbacks for all claim flow screens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all screen wrappers in RootNavigator to properly wire navigation callbacks so the claim flow works end to end.
acceptanceCriteria: OwnershipVerificationScreen wrapper: receives homeId and isTransfer from route params, onVerified navigates to ClaimConfirmation with homeId, onBack calls navigation.goBack()
ClaimConfirmationScreen wrapper: receives homeId from route params, onContinue navigates to Main tab (MyHome tab)
AddressEntryScreen: 'Claim This Home' button navigates to OwnershipVerification with homeId
AddressEntryScreen: 'Are you the new owner?' navigates to OwnershipVerification with homeId and isTransfer: true
MyHomeScreen: unclaimed CTA navigates to OwnershipVerification
MyHomeScreen: claimed badge navigates to RegistryDetail
Back navigation works correctly from all new screens
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement outbound data sync (app → registry)
userStories.description: As a homeowner, when I add equipment or complete assessments in the app, I want that data to sync back to the registry so my registry profile stays current.
acceptanceCriteria: In RegistryContext: add sync logic that watches for changes to equipment (via useEquipment), completed assessments (via AsyncStorage @pearl_completed_assessments)
After claiming, equipment additions trigger syncAppData(homeId, { equipment }) call
Assessment completions trigger syncAppData(homeId, { completedAssessments }) call
Sync is fire-and-forget: failures logged to console but do NOT disrupt app UX or show errors to user
Sync is debounced: batch changes, sync at most every 30 seconds using useRef timer
Mock service console.logs each sync call with payload summary for development visibility
Typecheck passes
